title: votive dedication to Aphrodite
support: A limestone slab, forming part of the front of a small votive table.

material._: marble, likely unspecified. Digital microscopy: indistinguishable fabric (identification based on digital microscopy)

layout.p: The text is set out over two lines on the lower part fo the front face of the slabe, in a field (4 cm high) demarcated from the upper part of the face by an incised horizontal line.

handNote.lettering: Small neat early Hellenistic letters, Alpha with straight bar, epsilon with short middle bar, zeta formed by vertical with horizontal bars top and bottom; Mu with four sloping strokes, full length; omicron, omega and theta all smaller than other letters; sigma with four equal strokes, top and bottom angled outwards; omega open with short horizontal tails.

provenanceFound._: First recorded in the College of the Jesuits in Palermo in 1748/1749 by Thomas Blackburne, but provenance not recorded. As a dedication to Aphrodite, it is tempting to attribute it to the sanctuary at Erice.

provenanceObserved._: observed in Palermo museum, but no proper autopsy to date

museum.name: Museo Archeologico Regionale Antonino Salinas
museum.description: Partially reopened following extensive restoration work. Comprehensive collection of archaeological material, predominantly from the West of the island, with an important Etruscan collection.

location.address: Centre of Palermo, Piazza Olivella 24, 90100 - Palermo
